current_suggestion.pitch: Your poker channels (GTO Wizard, GTO Lab, Jared Alderman) are heavy on solver-based GTO theory, but Doug Polk balances high-level strategy with real game application, hand history breakdowns, and honest discussion of how GTO concepts actually hold up at the table against human opponents. He covers spots where solver outputs require practical interpretation rather than rote application, which is a gap in pure theory content. Format is a mix of solo analysis, hand reviews, and occasional interviews, generally at an intermediate-to-advanced level.
